channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok rolling out age restrictions on beauty filters amid mental health concerns
item.description: TikTok announced Tuesday it will restrict the use of some appearance effects for users under 18 after teens and parents in a new study expressed concerns about the impact of beauty filters.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: X claims ownership of Infowars accounts
item.description: X, the social platform owned by Elon Musk, is getting involved in the pending bankruptcy sale of conspiracy theorist Alex Jones’s Infowars to the satirical newspaper The Onion.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: The key to operational AI: Modern data architecture
item.description: From customer service chatbots to marketing teams analyzing call center data, the majority of enterprises—about 90% according to recent data—have begun exploring AI. However, there’s a significant difference between those experimenting with AI and those fully integrating it into their operations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The iPhone 17 Air could have echoes of the original MacBook Air, if the latest rumors are true
item.description: The iPhone 17 Air will have its fair share of drawbacks, but it could be the start of a new era for Apple.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: AMD Takes On Nvidia: MI300 Series Revolutionizes AI Hardware Market
item.description: AMD's MI300 series aims to compete with Nvidia's GPUs, offering high-performance capabilities at a lower price point for real-time data and complex modeling applications.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Windows 11 Recall is misbehaving in testing, and the fix for the biggest issue is an age-old favorite: turn it off, then back on again
item.description: Microsoft suggests a true old chestnut – the one you should never contact IT support without trying first.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: La IA facilita la labor investigadora de los científicos del Instituto Curie
item.description: Ciencia, investigación y descubrimiento. El Instituto Curie se fundó en 1909 y se estableció como un laboratorio de excelencia dirigido por la científica Marie Curie, una mujer pionera en su campo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: La Junta de Andalucía potencia el desarrollo de proyectos de digitalización mediante metodologías ágiles
item.description: La Sociedad Andaluza para el Desarrollo de las Telecomunicaciones (Sandetel) se apoya en la filial tecnológica del grupo Indra, Minsait, con el propósito de potenciar el desarrollo de proyectos de digitalización mediante metodologías ágiles y eficientes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Windows Recall: All your privacy questions answered
item.description: Windows Recall is an AI-based feature that will be built into new Copilot+ PCs. Recall takes snapshots of your screen every few seconds and uses that data to make a searchable index of everything you’ve ever done on your PC.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Kotlin for Java developers: Concurrency with coroutines
item.description: The ability to perform multiple operations simultaneously is one of the most important aspects of any programming language. Orchestrating multiple paths of execution is inherently complex and there are various approaches to taming this complexity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal investigators in New York are seeking records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed this summer in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Shining a light on shadow AI: Three ways to keep your enterprise safe
item.description: To mitigate shadow AI, leaders should build cross-functional teams to leverage interdisciplinary expertise and foster organizational awareness. The post Shining a light on shadow AI: Three ways to keep your enterprise safe first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How to (kindly) ask people not to post your kids' photos on social media
item.description: With kids facing a seemingly increasing amount of threats from creeps on the internet, here's how you can respectfully ask people not to post photos of your children on social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At the Pacific Northwest National Laboratory automated testing is part of the design
item.description: Paul Reichlin director of digital platforms at the Pacific Northwest National Laboratory sees automated software testing as a foundational role. The post At the Pacific Northwest National Laboratory automated testing is part of the design first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal Agencies Embracing Artificial Intelligence PCs - A Game Changer in Decision-Making
item.description: AI PCs are revolutionizing decision-making at the edge for federal agencies, empowering them to address challenges with unprecedented speed and accuracy.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: This free time-zone conversion tool blows other clock apps out of the water
item.description: You’d think by this point in our lives, we’d have the whole time-zone-conversion thing figured out. It isn’t all that complicated, right?! And yet—well, trying to keep such calculations straight often feels like performing advanced algebra.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Surveillance tech advances by Biden could aid in Trump's promised crackdown on immigration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration — among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Garance Burke And Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cisco, NTT Data partner to simplify private 5G connectivity
item.description: Cisco and NTT Data are extending their partnership to offer customers more streamlined options for deploying private 5G services for enterprise connectivity. The partnership ties Cisco’s networking technologies to NTT Data’s Transatel network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Instagram's Threads gets 35 million new users this month
item.description: Nearly 35 million new users have flocked to Instagram's Threads this month as the Meta platform seeks to capture users leaving X and keep an edge over emerging platform Bluesky.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: UK Supreme Court hears landmark legal challenge over how a 'woman' is defined in law
item.description: The U.K. Supreme Court has begun hearing a legal challenge focusing on the definition of “woman” in a long-running dispute between a women’s right campaign group and the Scottish government.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Hui, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lost access? Here’s how to reclaim your Facebook account
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how to regain your Facebook access if you get hacked or locked out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Interpol clamps down on cybercrime and arrests over 1,000 suspects in Africa
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Interpol arrested 1,006 suspects in Africa during a massive two-month operation, clamping down on cybercrime that left tens of thousands of victims, including some who were trafficked, and produced millions in financial damages, the global police organization said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Don't chance it. Secure your retirement accounts with a VPN and protect your future
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to safeguard retirement accounts from cyber threats with a VPN: encryption, anonymity, secure access.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
